name: Poetic Edda
shortDescription: The Poetic Edda is a collection of Old Norse anonymous poems, primarily preserved in the Icelandic medieval manuscript Codex Regius. It is a crucial source for understanding Norse mythology and heroic legends.
description: The Poetic Edda, also known as the Elder Edda, is a collection of Old Norse poems primarily preserved in the Icelandic manuscript Codex Regius. Unlike the Prose Edda of Snorri Sturluson, which is a textbook of sorts aimed at teaching skaldic poetry, the Poetic Edda is a compilation of poems, largely anonymous, that provide invaluable insights into Norse mythology, heroic legends, and pre-Christian Germanic beliefs. The poems are diverse in style and content, ranging from mythological narratives about the creation of the world, the deeds of the gods, and the coming of Ragnarok, to heroic lays recounting the exploits of legendary figures like Sigurd (Siegfried) and Helgi. Key poems include the *Völuspá* (Prophecy of the Seeress), which details the creation and destruction of the world; the *Hávamál* (Sayings of the High One), a collection of wisdom verses attributed to Odin; and the *Þrymskviða* (Lay of Thrym), a humorous tale of Thor's missing hammer. The Poetic Edda offers a glimpse into the worldview, values, and religious beliefs of the Viking Age Scandinavians, and it continues to be a significant source of inspiration for literature, art, and scholarship related to Norse mythology.

sources.title: Norse Mythology: A Guide to the Gods, Heroes, Rituals, and Beliefs
sources.author: John Lindow
